title: Planet Labs Q2 2025 Earnings Highlights and Earnings Call Transcript
text: Planet Labs exceeded expectations with revenue of $73.4 million, representing 20% year-over-year growth. The company achieved an EPS of -$0.03, beating forecasts of -$0.05. Most notably, the firm posted adjusted EBITDA of $6.4 million, marking three consecutive profitable quarters, and generated $54.3 million in free cash flow. Defense and intelligence revenues accelerated 41% year-over-year, fueled by both core data solutions and the JSAT satellite services contract. The company's backlog surged 245% to $736.1 million, providing strong revenue visibility. Planet Labs announced it now expects to achieve free cash flow positivity for the full fiscal year, surpassing prior targets by over a year. CEO Will Marshall emphasized the company's unique positioning: Planet Labs PBC is the only space company that's truly central to AI. The firm secured major wins including a €240 million multi-year satellite services deal with Germany and expanded contracts with NATO and the U.S. Department of Defense for maritime domain awareness. For Q3, Planet Labs projects revenue between $71-74 million. Full fiscal year 2026 guidance increased to $281-289 million in revenue.
